nodes.label: supabase/migrations/ (12 SQL migrations)
nodes.rationale: Migrations 0011 and 0012 use IF NOT EXISTS / DROP POLICY IF EXISTS + CREATE / ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING so they're safe to re-run (idempotent).

nodes.label: src/app/actions/telegram.ts
nodes.rationale: Bot tokens stored per-user in user_preferences (not app-level env) so users bring their own Telegram bot; token read server-side only, never reaches the browser.

nodes.label: slide-renderer.tsx (25 template components)
nodes.rationale: Deliberate single-file design: templates share helpers (BaseSlide, BrandOverlay, fs(), fontMap); splitting would require a registry file + imports per template.

nodes.label: db-mappers.ts
nodes.rationale: Isolates the DB schema from the frontend; the frontend never sees UUIDs for templates/palettes, only slugs and p1-p4 IDs.

nodes.label: lib/logger.ts
nodes.rationale: Zero-dep logger: no pino/winston dependency, 8 colored tags, suppressed in production except ERROR.

nodes.label: lib/supabase/server.ts (createClient/createAdminClient)
nodes.rationale: Service-role key is server-only; createAdminClient() is used exclusively for account deletion, all other server ops use the publishable key with RLS.

nodes.label: Auth Flow
nodes.rationale: Cookie-based sessions via @supabase/ssr - no client-side token management; middleware refreshes session on every request.

nodes.label: Medical Mode
nodes.rationale: The app is repositioned as a medical/health content tool; non-medical features are feature-flagged off (not removed) so they can be restored by toggling NEXT_PUBLIC_MEDICAL_MODE.

nodes.label: Single-file Template System
nodes.rationale: All 25 templates live in one ~2300-line file by design, trading file size for shared-helper simplicity over a registry+imports pattern.

nodes.label: DB <-> Frontend Mapping
nodes.rationale: db-mappers.ts bridges two representations (DB enums/UUIDs vs frontend slugs/strings) so the frontend never depends on DB internals.

nodes.label: Two-pass AI generation
nodes.rationale: Content generation + medical accuracy review are separate AI calls; the review is non-blocking (failure defaults to needs_review, not blocked).

nodes.label: Server Actions for all mutations
nodes.rationale: No API routes; all writes go through "use server" functions with Zod validation + auth checks, keeping mutations Next.js-native and simple.

nodes.label: 0010_seed_new_styles.sql - Seeds 10 new style templates + 40 palette rows
nodes.norm_label: 0010_seed_new_styles.sql - seeds 10 new style templates + 40 palette rows

nodes.label: 0011_medical_wedge.sql - Medical layer: 4 new tables, modified tables, RLS, seeds
nodes.rationale: Idempotent migration (IF NOT EXISTS, DROP POLICY IF EXISTS + CREATE, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) so it is safe to re-run in the Dashboard.
nodes.norm_label: 0011_medical_wedge.sql - medical layer: 4 new tables, modified tables, rls, seeds

nodes.label: 0012_telegram_settings.sql - Adds telegram columns to user_preferences
nodes.rationale: Idempotent migration, extends export_records CHECK constraint for 'telegram' type without breaking re-runs.

nodes.label: Generated types gap (medical layer missing)
nodes.rationale: src/types/database.ts does not include the medical layer tables or migration 0011/0012 columns (except telegram columns); code uses `any` types in db-mappers.ts to bridge the gap.

nodes.label: Testing approach
nodes.rationale: No test framework is configured; verification is manual (typecheck, build, manual flow testing).

nodes.label: Ponytail convention (minimal comments, one runnable check)
nodes.rationale: Comments limited to '// ponytail:' for deliberate simplifications; non-trivial logic should leave one runnable assert-based check (not yet applied in this codebase).
nodes.norm_label: ponytail convention (minimal comments, one runnable check)
